operating_state: NIKE revenues increased 4% to $12.7 billion driven by North America, EMEA and APLA growth, partially offset by Greater China COVID-19 disruptions. Currency-neutral revenue growth reached 10% with NIKE Direct representing 42% of total revenues. Footwear revenues increased driven by Men's and Jordan Brand strength while apparel grew primarily from Men's category expansion.
cost_structure: Gross margin contracted 220 basis points to 44.3% primarily from elevated freight and logistics costs, higher promotional activity in North America, and unfavorable foreign currency impacts. Product costs increased approximately 190 basis points on wholesale equivalent basis while promotional activity reduced margins by 90 basis points. Selling and administrative expenses increased 10% to 30.9% of revenues driven by higher wage-related costs and strategic technology investments.
material_events: Company completed sale of Chile entity to third-party distributor with Argentina and Uruguay sales completed post-quarter, transitioning Central and South America to full distributor model. Board authorized new four-year $18 billion share repurchase program replacing terminated $15 billion program and repurchased 9.0 million shares for $991 million during the quarter. Nike appointed two new directors with shareholders approving amended Employee Stock Purchase Plan authorizing additional 11 million Class B shares.
macro_exposures: Inventory transit times improved ahead of plan causing seasonal inventory flow disruptions and North America inventory growth. Foreign currency headwinds increased significantly with USD strengthening reducing reported revenues by $823 million. Supply chain volatility continues creating challenges in managing seasonal product flow and elevated promotional activity across retail industry.
forward_outlook: Management expects unfavorable foreign currency impacts and continued elevated freight costs plus increased promotional activity to negatively impact Q2 gross margins. Company plans increased promotional activity in Q2 to sell excess inventory and create marketplace capacity for seasonally relevant products. Consumer Direct Acceleration strategy continues targeting long-term financial goals through digital transformation.
uncertainty_profile: Worsening macroeconomic conditions could drive higher inventory levels, increased obsolescence reserves, reduced consumer demand, and wholesale customer order cancellations. Inflation Reduction Act introduces fifteen percent minimum tax effective June 2023 and one percent excise tax on share repurchases after December 2022. Potential new COVID-19 restrictions across geographies remain unresolved contingencies affecting operations.
